title: Vallaris indecora

This family includes over 5,000 species of plants, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. Vallaris indecora is native to tropical regions of Asia and is often found in forests and grasslands. It is a climbing plant with fragrant white flowers and is used in traditional medicine in some parts of its range. Vallaris indecora is a member of the genus Vallaris, which is characterized by its woody vines and showy flowers.

intro: Vallaris indecora, also known as the "Butterfly Tree," is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
